title: V6 processors receive a flat OperationWithContext list via onOperations instead of grouped strands via onStrands
content: The v6 Reactor replaced `onStrands(strands: InternalTransmitterUpdate[])` with `onOperations(operations: OperationWithContext[])`. Each OperationWithContext destructures as `{ operation, context }` where context has `documentId`, `documentType`, `scope`, `branch`, `ordinal` (global monotonic counter for cross-document ordering), and optional `resultingState` (JSON string). Operation access changed: `operation.type` -> `operation.action.type`, `operation.input` -> `operation.action.input`, `operation.timestamp` -> `operation.action.timestampUtcMs`. The v6 processor manager guarantees exactly-once delivery -- no replay -- eliminating the need for `clearSource` / `index === 0` guards. Factory signature changed from `(driveId: string)` to `(driveHeader: PHDocumentHeader, processorApp?: ProcessorApp)` with outer parameter changing from direct dependencies to `IProcessorHostModule`.
description: The IProcessor interface simplified from strand-based to flat operation-based with per-operation context including global ordinal.
